mappings.rationaleAI: Periodic table maps to systematic classification (A), element groups (B), synthetic elements (C.5), and elemental origins (D).
Chemical elements cover classification (A), hydrogen and water (B.1), noble gases (B.9), metals/nonmetals (C.1), and origins (D, D.3).
Foundational chemistry maps to classification (A), element groups (B), and metals/nonmetals (C.1).
Chemistry history maps to periodic law (A) and element groups (B).
Quantum mechanics and bonding connect to classification (A), groups (B), and metals/nonmetals (C.1).
Nuclear foundations connect to classification (A), radioactive elements (C.2), synthetic elements (C.5), and stellar origins (D.3).
Stable and radioactive isotopes connect to element classification (C.2) and abundances (D, D.2).
Radioisotopes connect to radioactive elements (C.2), synthetic elements (C.5), and Earth abundances (D.1).
Water's properties map to hydrogen and water (B.1) and hydrosphere abundances (D.1.b).
Carbon-based molecular structures connect to the carbon group (B.5).
Metals, ceramics, and polymers connect to element groups (B), metals/nonmetals (C.1), and technologically significant elements (C.7).
Mineral families connect to native/combined elements (C.3) and crustal abundances (D.1.a).
Chemistry of living cells connects to biologically active elements (C.6).
Nuclear fission of uranium connects to radioactive elements (C.2) and synthetic elements (C.5).
Stellar nucleosynthesis maps to elemental origins in stars (D.3).
Solar fusion connects to stellar origins of elements (D.3).
Big Bang connects to origin of elements (D).
Earth science connects to elemental abundance on Earth (D.1).
Earth's structure connects to elemental abundance on Earth (D.1).
Atmospheric composition connects to atmospheric elemental abundance (D.1.c).
Earth systems connect to elemental abundance on Earth (D.1) and in the biosphere (D.1.d).
Solar System composition connects to elemental abundance in the solar system (D.2).

Covers physics of matter from elementary particles through atoms to galactic structures, tracing origins to the Big Bang — directly relevant to systematic classification of elements and their abundances in stars.
Covers the chemistry of carbon-containing compounds including bonding theory and functional groups — directly relevant to the carbon group of elements.
Covers chemical element synthesis in stars and galactic evolution — directly relevant to the origin and abundance of elements in the stars.
Covers the chemical composition of Earth's crust through rock types — relevant to occurrence of elements in the crust.
